verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a single-pass type-I transmembrane protein that rapidly cycles between the trans-Golgi network and the plasma membrane. Although its steady-state localization is at the TGN, it reaches the cell surface via documented CARTS carriers and maintains a dynamic, wash-resistant presence at the plasma membrane as an anchored integral membrane protein. The protein has a functional endocytic signal (YQRL motif) that drives retrieval from the PM back to the TGN, maintaining steady-state TGN localization despite continuous trafficking. This constitutive cycling pattern qualifies as dual localization with stable membrane anchoring—a contextual surface presentation.
